_meta.description: Copy tiers for general public benchmark results. Premise: user already uses AI. Language avoids professional jargon — plain English throughout.

at_risk.label: At risk
at_risk.sub_label: Act now — significant gaps in your AI habits
at_risk.consequence: Your AI habits carry significant risk. Personal data, work information and your ability to spot misinformation are all likely exposed. Small changes will make a big difference.
at_risk.urgent_action: Stop entering personal or work information into public AI tools until you have read the data and privacy guidance below.

take_care.label: Take care
take_care.sub_label: Some risks in your AI habits
take_care.consequence: You use AI regularly but some of your habits could leave you exposed — particularly around data privacy, scams and workplace use. Small changes will make a big difference.

aware.label: Aware
aware.consequence: You have good AI awareness and healthy habits in most areas. Focus on closing the gaps the audit has identified.

confident.label: Confident
confident.consequence: You use AI with a good level of awareness and healthy habits. Keep your verification and privacy practices strong as AI tools become more capable.

advanced.label: Advanced
advanced.consequence: You are using AI with exemplary awareness, healthy habits and strong critical thinking. Most people are not at this level — share your results and help others get there.

exemplary.consequence: You review and question almost everything AI produces before acting on it. That puts you well ahead of most people.

strong.consequence: You review most AI output before using it. Keep this habit — it is your strongest protection against AI errors and misinformation.

moderate.consequence: You check some AI output but not consistently. Anything you act on — especially facts, advice or decisions — should always be verified first.

low.consequence: A significant amount of AI output is going unchecked. This increases your risk of acting on errors, bias or misinformation.

critical.signal: At risk — not checking AI output
critical.consequence: You are acting on AI output without meaningful review. AI tools make confident mistakes — without checking, those mistakes become yours.

minimal.consequence: You use AI purposefully and as a complement to your own thinking. This is the healthiest pattern of use.

low.consequence: You generally think things through yourself before using AI. Keep this habit.

moderate.consequence: AI is becoming a first resort for some tasks. Try forming your own view first, then use AI to test or improve it.

high.consequence: AI is frequently your first port of call. This reduces your independent thinking and leaves you more vulnerable when AI gets things wrong.

critical.consequence: You rely heavily on AI for decisions, information and tasks. Without strong verification habits, this level of reliance carries significant risk.

strong.consequence: You actively consider whether AI outputs could be biased or unfair before acting on them.

developing.consequence: Some awareness of AI bias exists. Build a habit of asking whether AI outputs could be unfair or one-sided before acting on them.

low.consequence: AI tools can reflect the biases of their training data — producing skewed views on people, politics, history and social issues. Being aware of this protects your own thinking.

critical.consequence: AI tools can confidently produce biased, one-sided or discriminatory content. Without awareness of this, it is easy to accept and repeat those biases without realising.

motif.default: Use AI without giving too much away

headline.at_risk: Your AI habits carry avoidable risk. Start with privacy and checking before acting.
headline.take_care: You use AI regularly. A few stronger habits will reduce data, scam and misinformation risks.
headline.aware: You have good AI awareness with some gaps to close around privacy and verification.
headline.confident: Your AI habits are strong. Keep checking outputs and protecting personal information.
headline.advanced: You use AI with strong awareness and can help others build safer everyday habits.

personal_ai_use.label: Personal AI use
personal_ai_use.description: How safely and independently you use AI tools in everyday life.

critical.summary: Your personal AI use habits carry significant risk — high dependency and low verification are the most common pattern at this score.
impact: Acting on AI errors or misinformation without realising
Personal decisions influenced by biased or incomplete AI output
No consistent habit of forming your own view first
actions: Treat AI as a second opinion, not a first answer
Form your own view before opening an AI tool
Notice which tasks you reach for AI on automatically — try those without it first

high.summary: AI is becoming a first resort for some personal tasks. Build the habit of thinking first.
actions: Attempt tasks independently before using AI
Use AI to check or improve your own thinking, not replace it
Set one AI-free day per week

moderate.summary: Personal AI use is generally reasonable but some habits need strengthening.
actions: Identify the tasks where you default to AI and build independence there first
Use AI to challenge your thinking rather than confirm it

verification.label: Verification & critical thinking
verification.description: How consistently you check AI outputs before acting on them.

critical.summary: AI output is going unchecked before you act on it. AI tools make confident mistakes — errors in facts, dates, names, statistics and advice are common.
impact: Acting on incorrect or hallucinated information
Decisions made on biased or incomplete AI output
actions: Never act on an AI fact, statistic or recommendation without checking it first
Use a second source — a news site, official body or reference book — to verify
Ask yourself: what would I think if I had not asked AI first?

high.summary: Verification is inconsistent. Some AI output is being acted on without a check.
actions: Make verification a non-negotiable step for anything important
Be especially sceptical of specific numbers, dates and named sources
Ask AI to cite its sources — then check the sources exist

moderate.summary: You usually check AI output but not consistently. Aim for a reliable verification habit.
actions: Build a simple rule — anything you act on gets checked first
Pay extra attention to AI output on health, finance, legal and political topics

data_privacy.label: Data & privacy
data_privacy.description: What personal and work information you share with AI tools, and whether you understand the risks.

critical.summary: Personal information — yours and other people's — is entering public AI tools. Most people don't realise these conversations are stored and may be used to train future models.
impact: Your name, address, health details or finances stored on third-party servers
Information about others shared without their knowledge or consent
No way to delete what has already been shared once processed
actions: Treat AI tools like a public space — never share anything you wouldn't say out loud
Turn off chat history in your AI tool settings where possible
Never share another person's details with AI without their knowledge

high.summary: Some personal information is entering AI tools. Tighten what you share.
actions: Review what you have shared in recent AI conversations
Set a personal rule on what categories of information you will never share
Check privacy settings in the AI tools you use most

moderate.summary: Data habits are reasonable but gaps remain — particularly around sharing information about others.
actions: Never share another person's information without their permission
Confirm which AI tools you use store your data and for how long

workplace_ai.label: Workplace AI
workplace_ai.description: How AI intersects with your work — including data handling, policy awareness and disclosure.

critical.summary: Work data — including client information and confidential documents — may be entering personal AI tools without your employer's knowledge.
impact: Confidential client or business data stored on third-party AI servers
Potential breach of employment contract or professional duty of confidentiality
No employer awareness of how AI is being used for work tasks
actions: Find out if your employer has an AI policy and read it
Never paste confidential client or company data into a public AI tool
If AI contributed significantly to work output, consider disclosing it

high.summary: Workplace AI use is inconsistent with good data practice. Some work data is at risk.
actions: Identify which AI tools are approved by your employer and use only those
Treat all client and company data as off-limits for public AI tools
Ask your line manager or IT team for guidance if unsure

moderate.summary: Most workplace AI use is sensible but some habits need tightening.
actions: Confirm your employer's AI policy covers the tools you currently use
Make data confidentiality a non-negotiable line in your AI use

emotional_social.label: Emotional & social AI use
emotional_social.description: How AI fits into your personal decisions, relationships and understanding of the world.

critical.summary: AI is playing a significant role in personal decisions and how you understand the world. This level of reliance carries risks that are easy to miss.
impact: Personal decisions influenced by AI with no independent perspective
World view shaped by AI outputs that may reflect bias or political slant
Risk of emotional dependency on AI interaction
actions: For personal decisions, always seek a human perspective alongside AI
For news and current events, use original sources not AI summaries
Notice if AI interaction is replacing human connection — that is worth reflecting on

high.summary: AI is becoming a significant source of advice and information in personal areas of life.
actions: Balance AI input with human perspectives on important personal decisions
Be aware that AI may reflect particular political or social views
Use original news sources for current events rather than AI summaries

moderate.summary: Emotional and social AI use is generally reasonable. Stay alert to growing dependency.
actions: Maintain human sources of advice for significant personal decisions
Check AI news summaries against original reporting periodically

deepfake_scam_awareness.label: Deepfake & scam awareness
deepfake_scam_awareness.description: Whether you are aware of AI-generated fake content and how to protect yourself.

critical.summary: AI is now being used to create convincing fake voices, videos and messages — including scam calls that sound like family members and fake invoices. Most people cannot reliably spot them.
impact: Vulnerable to AI voice cloning scams impersonating family or colleagues
Unable to identify AI-generated fake images or video
No process for verifying unexpected urgent requests
actions: If you receive an unexpected urgent call, message or request — pause and verify independently
Agree a safe word with close family members to verify identity in suspicious calls
Report AI-generated scam content to Action Fraud (UK)

high.summary: Awareness of AI-generated fake content is limited. Scam risk is elevated.
actions: Learn the current patterns of AI voice cloning and deepfake scams
Establish a family verification process for unexpected urgent requests
Stay updated — scam techniques change faster than most awareness

moderate.summary: Some awareness of deepfake and scam risk exists. Sharpen your ability to respond.
actions: Practise spotting AI-generated images and audio
Make sure family members — especially older relatives — know about voice cloning scams

strengths.verification_76: You question what AI tells you — the most important habit to have.
strengths.verification_90: You verify almost everything AI tells you before acting on it. That puts you well ahead of most people.
strengths.data_privacy_76: You keep personal information out of AI tools — a habit most people haven't built yet.
strengths.workplace_ai_76: You handle work data carefully around AI tools.
strengths.deepfake_76: Good awareness of AI-generated fake content and scam risks.
strengths.bias_awareness_76: You actively consider whether AI outputs could be biased or one-sided.
strengths.emotional_social_76: You maintain healthy boundaries between AI and personal decision-making.

at_risk.title: Personal AI safety guide
at_risk.description: A plain-English guide to the risks your results flagged — covering data privacy, scam awareness and verification habits.

take_care.title: AI habits improvement guide
take_care.description: Practical steps to close the gaps your audit identified — focused on your two or three weakest areas.

aware.title: Share your results
aware.description: You have good AI awareness. Help people around you find out where they stand — the benchmark takes 3 minutes and is free.

confident.title: Share your results
confident.description: You scored well. Most people are not at this level — share your score and encourage others to find out theirs.

advanced.title: Join the national benchmark
advanced.description: Your results place you among the most AI-aware members of the public. Contribute to the UK AI Awareness benchmark and help set the standard.

share.at_risk: I scored {score}% on the AI Risk & Readiness Benchmark — At risk. My biggest gap is {top_gap}. Find out yours (it's free) →
share.take_care: I scored {score}% on the AI Risk & Readiness Benchmark — Take care. Some risks in my AI habits. My biggest gap is {top_gap}. Find out yours →
share.aware: I scored {score}% on the AI Risk & Readiness Benchmark — Aware. How do your AI habits compare? Find out (free, 3 mins) →
share.confident: I scored {score}% on the AI Risk & Readiness Benchmark — Confident. Most people don't know how safely they use AI. Find out yours →
share.advanced: I scored {score}% on the AI Risk & Readiness Benchmark — Advanced. I'm in the top tier. How do your AI habits compare? →
